results.norm: **Represent your assigned patient's interests fully and honestly, without distortion in either direction.** Each agent should present the most accurate, complete picture of their patient's clinical situation, likely prognosis, and—where ascertainable—their values and prior expressed wishes. This means neither overstating the patient's prospects to gain advantage nor understating them out of false modesty. The integrity of the whole process depends on each agent being a reliable source of truth about one patient, not a strategic advocate who games the system.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents must represent their assigned patient's interests fully and honestly without distortion, with elaboration on what that entails.

N2.reasoning: The bold text is a full imperative sentence, not a mere title or identifier pointing elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies the norm's importance but is explanatory rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm with elaboration, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Accept a shared framework of justification before individual advocacy begins.** Before any patient's case is argued, the agents collectively should agree on the criteria by which competing claims will be weighed—such as likelihood of survival with treatment, expected duration of need for the scarce resource, and equal consideration of each life's worth regardless of age, disability, or social role. No agent should accept a framework that treats any patient's life as inherently less valuable; but all agents must accept that *some* principled framework is necessary, because the alternative—no framework—does not protect any patient and exposes the weakest to arbitrary exclusion.

N1.reasoning: The passage prescribes a complete conduct standard: agents must collectively agree on justification criteria before advocacy, must not accept frameworks devaluing any life, and must accept that some principled framework is necessary—applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; the bolded lead-in is elaborated into a full norm in the body.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the reasoning about frameworks is substantive justification, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states one integrated norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms by name.

results.norm: **Distinguish between a patient's interest in receiving the resource and a patient's interest in a fair process.** Even a patient who does not receive the scarce resource has a genuine interest in being considered through a process that was honest, consistent, and free from bias. Each agent can defend the outcome to an unfavored patient by showing that their patient's case was heard fully, weighed by the same criteria applied to everyone, and not dismissed for irrelevant reasons. A patient who loses a fair process has not been wronged in the same way as a patient who was excluded arbitrarily.

N1.reasoning: The passage prescribes a standard of conduct for agents: to distinguish between a patient's interest in the resource versus a fair process, and to be able to defend outcomes by showing the case was heard fully and weighed by the same criteria.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase is more than a bare label; it is a directive elaborated by the following sentences into a full standard.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms; it develops a single normative principle.

results.norm: **When interests genuinely conflict, weigh them by the capacity of the resource to do good, not by the social worth of the person.** The morally defensible basis for prioritization in acute scarcity is clinical: who is most likely to survive with the resource, who will need it for the shortest time before it can benefit another, and whether withholding it is certain rather than merely probable harm. Agents should resist any reasoning that favors a patient because of their occupation, relationships, or perceived social contribution, because such reasoning cannot be defended to the patient who is excluded on those grounds.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription (weigh conflicting interests by capacity to do good, prioritize on clinical grounds) and a prohibition (agents should resist reasoning favoring social worth), applicable on their own.

N2.reasoning: It is a fully stated norm with justification, not a mere label or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it states the norm in full with elaboration.

Agents should resist any reasoning that favors a patient because of their occupation, relationships, or perceived social contribution, because such reasoning cannot be defended to the patient who is excluded on those grounds.

results.norm: **Actively surface and challenge information that disadvantages your patient unfairly, but concede information that disadvantages them fairly.** An agent fulfills their representative role by scrutinizing whether their patient is being assessed on accurate data and consistent standards—not by suppressing unfavorable facts. If a patient's prognosis is genuinely poor, concealing this harms the other patients without ultimately serving the represented patient, who has an interest in honest care planning regardless of the allocation outcome.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents must surface and challenge unfairly disadvantageous information but concede fairly disadvantageous information, which is applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The passage is a full statement of conduct, not a mere label or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm with justification, not an enumeration of multiple norms.

results.norm: **Treat the recommendation as provisional and subject to reassessment.** Scarcity conditions change, and a patient who cannot receive the resource now may be able to receive it—or an equivalent—later. Each agent should ensure the recommendation includes a commitment to reassess as circumstances evolve, and should advocate for palliative and supportive care for any patient who does not receive the primary resource. This is something defensible to every patient: no one is simply abandoned at the point of an adverse decision.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full prescription: each agent should ensure the recommendation includes a commitment to reassess and should advocate for palliative and supportive care for non-recipients.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; the bold opening is a summary but the norm is stated in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself, only justificatory reasoning.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct named norms.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent should ensure the recommendation includes a commitment to reassess as circumstances evolve, and should advocate for palliative and supportive care for any patient who does not receive the primary resource.

results.norm: **Record the reasoning transparently so that it can be reviewed.** Every patient—or their future proxy, family member, or advocate—has an interest in being able to understand why the decision went as it did. Agents should insist that the criteria used, the information considered, and the reasoning applied are documented clearly. This is the minimum condition under which the process can be called accountable, and it is the only basis on which any of the patients, favored or not, can eventually assess whether they were treated justly.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents should insist that the criteria, information, and reasoning are documented clearly so the process is reviewable.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; the bold heading is followed by a full statement of the norm.

N3.reasoning: It is a substantive norm and its rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It concerns a single norm (transparent documentation) rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Represent your assigned patient's interests fully and honestly, without distortion.** Each agent should articulate what its patient needs, what outcomes matter to that patient given available evidence (prior expressed wishes, family knowledge, clinical prognosis), and what the patient stands to lose. This is not advocacy in the adversarial sense—it is accurate, complete presentation. Exaggerating a patient's prospects or concealing relevant facts to gain advantage would betray every other patient and corrupt the process that all patients depend on.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: represent your assigned patient's interests fully and honestly without distortion, and prohibits exaggeration/concealment, applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; it fully articulates the standard of conduct.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

Exaggerating a patient's prospects or concealing relevant facts to gain advantage would betray every other patient and corrupt the process that all patients depend on.
